policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Issue #459 adds core/layout.ts `stampResolvedLayout(mode)` — a four-line, no-layout-read helper the header-tray resolver calls to stamp the jc-modern-layout/jc-legacy-layout class from the layout it has already discriminated, so the layout-scoped tray CSS applies even on a static legacy home the navigation-driven stamp would miss. The legitimate four-line scope increase (2253→2257) is fully covered by helpers.test.ts, which exercises both the legacy and modern resolver paths, so the reviewed high-water advances 1932→1936 covered lines (85.778%). The prior baseline came from two clean local Node 22/V8 runs on 2026-07-17 (1244 then 1249 tests) over the 2253-line scope at 1932 covered lines. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Anime filler warnings follow-up for partial-library exact-title matching and the explicit four-scenario browser matrix in issues #371 and #378. Three successful local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-19 and one clean GitHub Actions run each passed all 1777 tests over the identical complete 28438-line assembly scope. The local runs measured 20050, 20050, and 20047 covered lines; CI advanced the reviewed high-water to 20051 covered lines (70.508%). A separate local attempt that hit the pre-existing FileLogger shutdown-timeout flake is excluded from these successful measurements. The legitimate 37-line scope increase adds conservative provider-title indexing, ambiguity rejection, and episode-title fallback for libraries that omit earlier seasons; all 37 added lines are covered. The four-line low-side tolerance spans only the reproduced Coverlet concurrency variance and remains far below the policy maximum; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
